dimensions.name: correctness
dimensions.evidence: `go run ./cmd/furrow handoff validate <invalid-driver.md> --json` returned `{"ok":true,"verdict":"valid"}` even when the handoff had `Step: NOT_A_REAL_STEP` and an invalid row, because `validateDriverMarkdown` only checks for marker presence.

dimensions.name: test-coverage
dimensions.evidence: `internal/cli/handoff/cmd.go` adds branching in `Handler.Run`, `runRenderDriver`, `runRenderEngine`, `runValidate`, and `parseRenderFlags`, but `rg` found no tests covering those command handlers or flag parsing.

dimensions.name: spec-compliance
dimensions.evidence: The CLI/validator surface diverges from the spec: `runRenderDriver` fabricates objective/grounding and always emits empty constraints instead of consuming D4/state/definition data, `runRenderEngine` only accepts stdin, and `ValidateFile` chooses by H1 marker rather than sniffing the rendered handoff target prefix/schema content.

dimensions.name: unplanned-changes
dimensions.evidence: The D1 commit also modified `go.mod` to promote `gopkg.in/yaml.v2` from indirect to direct, but that file is outside the obvious handoff deliverable surface and the commit summary does not justify the change.

dimensions.name: code-quality
dimensions.evidence: `internal/cli/handoff/validate.go` duplicates schema logic in ad hoc Go checks instead of validating against the checked-in JSON Schema files, leaving multiple sources of truth and already causing acceptance of invalid rendered driver handoffs.
